name: One Pit · Three Roles
source: Chris Atkinson · Y3–Y4 long jump session card

ks1.objective: Children experience the full jump with a defined safety role for every group member.
successCriteria: Jumpers take off on one foot
Pit Crew complete their assigned role

lks2.objective: Every child jumps 4–5 times into the pit with focused observation and active safety roles. Apply the TALL · PUSH · LAND framework.
successCriteria: 5–7 stride run-up, one-foot take-off, controlled landing
Returns via side route (never across runway)
Pit Crew complete role for every jumper
Watchers report one observation per rotation

uks2.objective: Refine the full jump with self-coaching, observation skills, and measurement awareness. Personal best challenge.
successCriteria: Self-reports one thing they did well + one to improve

equipment.item: Cones — start line, take-off marker, lane edges

space.description: Standard long-jump runway and sand pit. Pre-check pit for stones, sticks, debris. Marked side route for jumpers to return. Coach position: at the take-off board, angled to see both runway and pit.

steps: Form 3 groups of 5. Assign roles: Group A = JUMPERS, B = WATCHING CREW, C = PIT CREW.
Brief each group: Jumpers = 5–7 stride run-up, one-foot take-off, land in pit, walk forward out, return via SIDE route.
Brief Watching Crew = stand well back from the take-off. All 5 watch for ONE same cue per rotation (coach announces it). Thumbs up/down per jumper.
Brief Pit Crew = five jobs, one child per job: Raker / Landing marker / Pit exit warden / Safety starter / Foot caller.
Coach announces watcher focus: Rotation 1 = TALL. Rotation 2 = RUN THROUGH (no braking). Rotation 3 = SNAP (quick take-off).
Safety Starter gives thumbs-up when pit is clear. Coach calls each jumper by name.
Jumper runs, jumps, lands, exits forward, returns via SIDE. ~18 sec turnaround. Each jumper goes 4–5 times in their rotation.
After ~4 min, ONE whistle = stop. TWO whistles = rotate clockwise: Jumpers → Pit Crew → Watching → Jumpers.
Three full rotations. Coach moves only between rotations.

cues.phrase: "Same speed all the way to the line"
"Feet to the front — show me your shoelaces!"

errors.cause: Chasing speed; or watching their feet
errors.fix: "Stand TALL — eyes on the far end of the pit."
"Foot like a hot floor — QUICK push, don't sink in." Refer back to Quick Feet.

errors.cause: Approach speed too low to load the knee drive
errors.fix: "DRIVE your front knee up — fast and high."

errors.issue: Arms by the side, no help
errors.fix: "PUNCH the sky with your opposite arm."
"FEET to the FRONT — show me your shoelaces."
"ONE foot for take-off — your STRONG foot."
"Same speed all the way to the line — don't BRAKE." Shorten the run-up if needed.

progressions: Support: shorter run-up — just 3 steps from a cone, walk-in start; use a 1m-wide take-off zone instead of a precise board; coach counts steps aloud; aim for a TALL FINISH not distance; pair with a confident peer.
Standard: 5–7 step run-up from a measured start, precise take-off board, one-foot take-off, controlled landing.

Stretch: hold the tall body in the air — slight hang shape; place a soft hurdle / cone above the board to encourage knee drive; personal-best challenge — beat your last jump by one cone-length; self-coach with a partner (one thing done well + one to improve); coach measures their best jump for next session.
questions: What does TALL mean for our body when we jump?
Why do we PUSH the ground quickly? (Remember the tennis ball!)
What do our feet do when we LAND so we don't fall back?
base.showmanship: Announce the WATCHER FOCUS at the start of each rotation. Whole group is now watching for one thing — shared purpose. Use the rotation as the engagement engine. Praise loudly, correct quietly. Every child has a specific job every jump.

watcherFocus: Rotation 2: RUN THROUGH the take-off (no brake)
pitCrewJobs: Landing marker — places a cone where heels landed
Pit exit warden — directs jumpers out the front and round the side
Safety starter — thumbs-up to coach when pit is clear
Foot caller — calls out which foot took off

languageRule: Two coaching words max per cue. Always positive. NEVER say 'don't' — say what you DO want.
praisePhrases: Lovely quick push — like the tennis ball!
Great big knee drive — I saw it!
That looked light and bouncy — try it again.
Eyes up next time and that'll be even bigger.
You stayed tall the whole way — that's the secret.
